source: Lee et al., 2019, ASTCT CRS grading; PMC11739781; Blinatumomab SmPC; PMC6853151 (Pfizer modeling)
description: Expected findings for bispecific T-cell engagers (CD3 x target format). CRS is the primary on-target expected toxicity — Grade 1-2 is expected pharmacology, Grade 3+ is adverse. Cynomolgus monkey used when molecule cross-reacts with NHP CD3 AND target antigen. Inherits Fc-mediated base findings from general_mab.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: Grade 1-2 CRS is expected on-target pharmacology. Grade 3+ (>50x with clinical signs, vasopressors required, refractory fever) is adverse — do not reclassify.
expected_findings.rationale: IL-6 is the key CRS cytokine and primary driver of fever and hypotension. Tocilizumab (anti-IL-6R) used to treat Grade 3+ CRS. (PMC11739781; Lee et al., ASTCT 2019)

expected_findings.severity_threshold: Correlates with CRS severity. HLH signature (IFN-gamma + extreme ferritin + cytopenias) = never reclassify.
expected_findings.rationale: IFN-gamma is the primary product of activated CD8+ T-cells; mediates many CRS manifestations. (PMC11739781)

expected_findings.description: CRP elevation (downstream of IL-6 in CRS)
expected_findings.severity_threshold: >100 mg/L with Grade 3 CRS clinical features = adverse
expected_findings.rationale: CRP elevates as downstream acute phase response to CRS IL-6 surge.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: Transient (<48h recovery) on first dose = expected. Sustained depletion >72h or Grade 3 CRS with lymphopenia = adverse.
expected_findings.rationale: T-cells undergo redistribution/margination during immune synapse formation; transient depletion is the primary pharmacological response, NOT toxicity. (Blinatumomab SmPC; PMC6853151)

expected_findings.typical_magnitude: Grade 1-2 (1-5x ULN) in context of CRS
expected_findings.severity_threshold: Grade 3 (>5x ULN) = adverse. Grade 4 (>20x ULN) = never reclassify.
expected_findings.rationale: Hepatic involvement in CRS/inflammatory cascade; Grade 3+ hepatotoxicity = adverse. (ASTCT CRS grading 2019)
Parallels ALT pattern in CRS context.
